question: What ASME code classification is the NuScale containment vessel (CNV) designed to?

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a containment vessel. The RPV is a critical barrier to fission product release and is constructed from materials specified by the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant’s design life. Material specifications and fabrication standards are detailed to maintain the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: The section describes the design features of each NuScale Power Module (NPM) that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant’s design life, in accordance with regulatory definitions. It details the specific components and systems included in the RCPB, such as the reactor pressure vessel, containment isolation valves, safety valves, and associated piping, emphasizing their roles in maintaining pressure containment and system safety.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components comply with relevant U.S. regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria. It details that these components are designed, fabricated, and tested to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) Section III standards, with specific classifications for different piping systems, and references the use of the 2013 Edition of the ASME BPVC as the code of record.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes key components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. It removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for degasification and emergency core cooling to maintain safety and integrity. The RCS interfaces with several other systems, including chemical control, emergency cooling, and sampling systems.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module (NPM), which integrates the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single reactor pressure vessel (RPV) surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a key pressure-retaining component and barrier to fission product release, supporting various reactor systems and constructed according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure integrity throughout the plant's design life. Material specifications for the RPV and its components are selected to maintain the reactor coolant pressure boundary's integrity.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: This section describes the materials and standards for threaded fasteners in the reactor coolant pressure boundary, emphasizing the use of Alloy 718 for compatibility and stress corrosion cracking mitigation. It also outlines that preservice and inservice inspection and testing of pressure-retaining components are conducted according to the latest ASME BPVC Section XI requirements, as mandated by federal regulations, with inspection intervals and code editions specified in the inspection program.

nuscale_ch05_0016.title: Pressure-Temperature Limits, Pressurized Thermal Shock, and Charpy Upper-Shelf Energy Data and Analyses
nuscale_ch05_0016.summary: This section outlines the methodology and regulatory compliance for establishing pressure-temperature operational limits for the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) over the plant’s 60-year lifespan. It describes how these limits are calculated using ASME and NRC guidelines, accounting for vessel embrittlement, and provides reference figures and tables for acceptable pressure-temperature combinations to prevent non-ductile failure of the reactor vessel.

edges.description: Section A describes the physical arrangement and components of the NuScale Power Module, including the containment vessel (CNV), while Section B defines the scope of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) and its components, establishing which vessels and piping are subject to regulatory classification—thus, Section A's description of the CNV is contextually linked to Section B's definition of what must meet ASME code classification.

Section A describes the containment vessel (CNV) as surrounding the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B specifies that the RPV and pressure retaining components are designed to ASME BPVC Section III, Class 1, but does not explicitly mention the CNV, connecting them through their discussion of vessel classification and code compliance.

Section A describes the physical arrangement and components of the NuScale Power Module, including the containment vessel (CNV), while Section B elaborates on the function of the reactor coolant system (RCS) and its pressure-retaining boundaries, but neither section specifies the ASME code classification for the CNV; rather, Section A provides context for the CNV's relationship to the RPV and RCS as referenced in Section B.

Section A defines the reactor coolant pressure boundary components, while Section B specifies that these components, including the containment vessel if classified as part of the RCPB, are designed to ASME BPVC Section III, Class 1 requirements.

Section A defines the scope and integrity requirements of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB), while Section B describes system features and components (including the CNV) that function to maintain the RCPB, thus providing supporting details for Section A's general requirements.

Section B describes the physical arrangement and components of the NuScale Power Module, including the containment vessel (CNV), while Section A defines the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) and its regulatory basis, establishing that the CNV is part of the RCPB as referenced in regulatory definitions.

Section A specifies that pressure-retaining components associated with the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB), which includes the containment vessel (CNV) as described in Section B, are designed to ASME BPVC Section III, Class 1 requirements.

Section A specifies that pressure retaining components associated with the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) are designed as ASME Class 1, while Section B clarifies that the containment vessel (CNV) surrounds the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) but does not explicitly state the CNV's ASME code classification, thus Section A provides the regulatory classification context for components described in Section B.

Section B describes the containment vessel (CNV) as part of the NuScale Power Module and references the RCS, which is described in Section A, establishing that Section B's details are structurally linked to Section A's system description.

Section A references the applicable ASME BPVC Sections II and III for material and design requirements of pressure boundary components, while Section B describes the containment vessel (CNV) as part of the NuScale Power Module but does not specify its ASME code classification, indicating that Section A's mention of ASME codes may apply to the CNV as well.

Section A specifies that threaded fasteners in the reactor pressure boundary conform to ASME BPVC Sections II and III, while Section B defines the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components, thereby connecting the ASME code requirements in A to the boundary components described in B.

Section A describes the materials and ASME BPVC Section II and III compliance for threaded fasteners in the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B states that the RPV and pressure retaining components (which include the containment vessel) are designed as ASME BPVC Section III, Class 1, thus Section A's details support Section B's broader regulatory compliance claim.

Section A references specific ASME BPVC sections for reactor vessel attachments and welds, while Section B describes the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components, connecting them through the regulatory requirement that RCPB components (such as the containment vessel) must comply with ASME code classifications.

Section A discusses specific ASME BPVC requirements for reactor vessel attachments, while Section B states that the reactor pressure vessel and pressure-retaining components are designed as ASME BPVC Section III, Class 1, thus Section A's details are governed by the overall code classification described in Section B.
